title: Phishing-Resistant Passwordless Login
description:
## A Hardware Security Key, No Password

You want to offer **phishing-resistant, passwordless** login: users authenticate with a **hardware security key** (or platform authenticator) and never type a password. Which Keycloak capability fits?

options.text: The **WebAuthn (passwordless)** authenticator, added to the authentication flow.
**TOTP** via an authenticator app, since one-time codes are passwordless.
**Brute force detection** to lock accounts after failed logins.

explanation: Keycloak supports **WebAuthn**, including a **passwordless WebAuthn** authenticator that you add as an execution in an authentication flow. WebAuthn uses public-key cryptography bound to the origin, which makes it **phishing-resistant** — credentials can't be replayed on a fake site — and the passwordless variant lets the security key fully replace the password. Option **b** is wrong: **TOTP** still relies on a **shared secret** and a code the user can be tricked into entering on a phishing page, so it is **not** phishing-resistant (and it's a second factor, not passwordless on its own). Option **c** strengthens passwords but keeps a phishable password. Option **d** is a defense against guessing attacks, not an authentication method, and doesn't make login passwordless or phishing-resistant.

hints.title: What makes it phishing-resistant?
hints.body: WebAuthn binds the credential to the site's origin, so a key registered for the real site won't authenticate against a look-alike. Shared-secret OTP has no such binding.
